title: Ground Turkey & Cottage Cheese Bowl Recipe (390 Cal, 44g Protein) | Eati
introduction: Ground Turkey & Cottage Cheese Bowl is a high-protein meal delivering 44g of protein and 390 calories per serving — designed for muscle building and fat loss. With 10g of fat and 22g of carbohydrates, it fits comfortably into any macro-tracked eating plan. Log it in Eati to track your macros automatically and stay on target.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- 200g ground turkey (93% lean)
- 150g low-fat cottage cheese
- 100g canned black beans, drained
- 50g salsa
- 1 tsp olive oil
- Cumin, paprika, garlic powder
- Salt, pepper
- Fresh cilantro and lime to serve

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey with cumin, paprika, garlic powder, salt.
2. Cook 7–8 minutes, breaking into small pieces, until cooked through.
3. Meanwhile, warm black beans in microwave 1 minute.
4. Assemble bowl: turkey base, black beans, cottage cheese on the side.
5. Top with salsa, cilantro, lime juice.
6. Log in Eati: 'ground turkey 200g, cottage cheese 150g, black beans 100g'.

sections.heading: Calories, Protein, Fat & Carbs
sections.content:
Per serving (estimated):

Calories: 390 kcal
Protein: 44g
Fat: 10g
Carbohydrates: 22g

Macro split: 45% protein / 23% fat / 23% carbs

Protein per 100 calories: 11.3g

Note: values are estimates based on typical ingredient weights. Log exact portions in Eati for precise tracking — the app calculates macros automatically from your description or a barcode scan.

sections.heading: Why Ground Turkey & Cottage Cheese Bowl Works for Muscle Gain and Weight Loss
sections.content:
Stacking two protein sources — ground turkey and cottage cheese — in one bowl is a technique that bodybuilders have used for decades. The result is 44g of protein in a 390-calorie meal that covers almost a third of an average daily protein target.

Macro breakdown (estimated):
• 390 calories
• 44g protein
• 10g fat
• 22g carbohydrates

Cottage cheese adds slow-digesting casein:
Cottage cheese is about 70% casein protein. Consumed with a fast-digesting protein like turkey, it creates a dual-release protein supply — rapid amino acid availability from turkey (60–90 min) and sustained release from casein (4–6 hours). This combination is particularly effective for muscle preservation during a calorie deficit.

sections.heading: Variations and Serving Ideas
sections.content:
1. Mexican bowl (410 cal, 44g protein): add jalapeño, corn, avocado.
2. Italian bowl (400 cal, 44g protein): season turkey with Italian herbs, replace beans with cherry tomatoes.
3. Low-carb (330 cal, 44g protein): skip beans, add extra salsa.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Storage & Time-Saving Tips
sections.content: Cook turkey in bulk (4 servings, 800g total). Store 4 days refrigerated. Portion cottage cheese separately, add fresh at serving. Beans store 4 days in fridge after draining.

conclusion: Ground Turkey & Cottage Cheese Bowl delivers 44g of protein in 390 calories — a macro profile that makes it one of the most effective dishes for body recomposition. Add it to your weekly rotation, track each serving in Eati, and let consistent protein intake do the work.

faqs.question: How many calories in a ground turkey and cottage cheese bowl?
faqs.answer: About 390 calories per serving — 44g protein, 10g fat, 22g carbs.

faqs.question: Can I mix cottage cheese with ground turkey?
faqs.answer: Yes — it's an effective protein stacking technique. The cottage cheese adds slow-digesting casein alongside turkey's faster protein, creating a dual-release effect that extends satiety.
